summary: Crystal‑clear Miller soundboard capture of the legendary 9/28/72 Stanley Theatre run, highlighted by a monster He's Gone, a soaring Other One and a beautiful Me & Bobby McGee.
review: The Miller SBD source delivers an exceptionally clean mix that lets every instrument shine, though the vocal track on Loser is missing and some AUD patches had to be pitch‑corrected. Musically this show is consistently praised as one of ’72’s finest – a blistering first set and a second set that erupts with He's Gone, The Other One, Me & Bobby McGee, Wharf Rat and a thunderous finish.

ai_rating.rationale: All ten reviews give five‑star ratings and focus on the show’s outstanding performances while noting only minor recording quirks (missing Loser vocals, patching). The consensus is overwhelmingly positive.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Miller SBD provides crisp, balanced sound; instruments are clearly audible. Minor issues: vocal track missing on Loser, occasional AUD patches needed pitch correction (+1‑4%).

standout_songs: China Cat > I Know You Rider
Greatest Story Ever Told (St. Stephen jam)
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is praised as one of the strongest of the period, with a high‑energy Truckin' opener and tight early cuts. The second set builds after He's Gone into an expansive Other One, then flows through Me & Bobby McGee, Wharf Rat and a powerful Sugar Magnolia/Not Fade Away close.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s lead work is described as "tiger stuff" on He's Gone and Not Fade Away; his tone and soloing receive repeated praise.
band_member_comments.Phil: His bass solo on He's Gone is called "sheets of sound" and one of the best ever, with funky high‑speed runs that draw comparisons to Coltrane.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bobby’s rhythm playing (UniVibe pedal) is highlighted as creative and dominant in the mix; his contribution to Me & Bobby McGee and overall groove is lauded.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith's presence is noted as low or occasionally absent, but still credited for great moments when heard.
